name: Drowned Ward Entry Restriction
publicDescription: Citizens are forbidden from entering the Drowned Ward due to structural instability and safety concerns.
dmSecret: The restriction exists less because of structural danger and more because the Ward contains evidence of pre-Atlantean ruins, hidden survivors, and old cover-ups.

name: Restricted Archives Act
publicDescription: Certain historical, military, and magical records are restricted for public safety and national security.
punishments: Reassignment or imprisonment in severe cases
dmSecret: This law is commonly used to bury investigations connected to Operation Tidefall, the Drowned Ward, and the original recruits.

name: Tidewarden Military Authority
publicDescription: Tidewardens possess authority to protect Atlantis, conduct investigations, secure restricted sites, and respond to threats throughout the Deep Realm.
dmSecret: This broad authority allows the Crown to quietly redirect or suppress investigations without appearing tyrannical.

name: Surface Trade Regulation
publicDescription: Merchant vessels must declare cargo, crew, docking intentions, and restricted goods before entering major ports.
dmSecret: Smugglers often exploit remote settlements and floating black markets outside major trade lanes.

name: Azure Sentinel Authority
publicDescription: Azure Sentinels may investigate threats, arrest criminals, inspect vessels, defend settlements, and enforce royal law throughout the Surface Realm.
dmSecret: Surface citizens generally trust the Azure Sentinels, but Atlanteans may view them with suspicion.

name: Piracy Prohibition
publicDescription: Unauthorized attacks against merchant vessels, civilian ships, fishing fleets, or trade routes are punishable by death.
dmSecret: Some pirate crews know forbidden routes and lost history that lawful governments would rather erase.

name: Smuggling Prohibition
publicDescription: Transporting restricted goods without proper permits is illegal throughout most civilized regions of Thalassar.
dmSecret: Smuggling routes through the Drowned Ward may connect to deeper conspiracy activity.

name: Ruin Claim Registration
publicDescription: Ancient ruins discovered by explorers, salvagers, or adventurers must be reported before excavation rights are granted.
dmSecret: This law becomes important when surface ruins are revealed to connect to Atlantis and pre-Atlantean history.

name: Monster Worship Ban
publicDescription: Organizations dedicated to destructive leviathans, abyssal horrors, eldritch entities, or world-ending threats are outlawed.
dmSecret: This law may eventually intersect with the Cult of the Sleeper Below.

name: Necromancy Regulation
publicDescription: Necromancy is heavily regulated. Animating unwilling dead and disturbing burial sites are forbidden.
dmSecret: Academic study may be permitted, which creates loopholes for dangerous research.

name: Dangerous Magic Restriction
publicDescription: Spells capable of causing large-scale destruction are regulated in major population centers.
dmSecret: Atlantis may classify certain historical magic as dangerous simply to prevent public study.
